steps.title: Qualify the moonlet success
steps.content: NASA successfully altered the orbit of a secondary object, a moonlet. The next clause says scientists have not yet shown that primary objects would be similarly affected, so the transition must qualify or contrast with the previous success.

steps.title: Reject additive transitions
steps.content: A signals an example, but the unproven primary-object case is not an example of the moonlet success. C signals similarity, which is exactly what remains unproven. D signals an additional supporting point, but the sentence limits the previous point. The answer is B.
